analysis_description: Round 3 tapes (days 0–2): Black–Scholes IV per VEV from top-of-book mid vs VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT; quadratic IV smile in m_t=log(K/S)/sqrt(T) with WLS weights 1+bid_vol_1+ask_vol_1 (volume conviction). T from round3description (8/7/6 dte at open for csv days 0/1/2) plus intraday wind dte_eff=dte_open-(timestamp//100)/10000; T_years=dte_eff/365; r=0 (same as round3work/plotting/original_method/combined_analysis/plot_iv_smile_round3.py). Greeks: vega at fitted IV for summary mean. Microstructure: mean top-of-book spread ~3.08 on included strikes.
used_to_decide: Residual IV is roughly mean-zero (mean -4.9e-5); std ~0.012 guides RESID_ENTER scale. Corr(|residual|, log(weight)) ≈ -0.11 supports weighting the smile by book size. Session-open extract mids 5250.0 / 5245.0 / 5267.5 used as backtest csv_day anchors when traderData resets per day.

analysis_description: Extended Greek-aware tape analysis on all timestamps for days 0–2: compute IV via BS inversion from top-of-book mid for each VEV, fit per-timestamp volume-WLS smile residual, then profile |residual| against delta, vega, volume, and spread by strike. Timing remains from round3description: day0/1/2 open DTE 8/7/6 with intraday wind dte_eff = dte_open - (timestamp//100)/10000.
used_to_decide: Residual quality is strike-dependent: VEV_5100..VEV_5500 have tight spreads (about 1.1 to 4.3) and manageable residual magnitudes, while VEV_4000/4500 are much wider (about 15.8 to 20.1) and noisy. This motivated v3/v4 to keep the same residual-fade thesis but restrict active trading to more liquid middle strikes.

analysis_description: Subsampled (timestamp step 500) first differences: VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT mid vs BS implied vol from VEV_5300 mid with K=5300, r=0, T from dte_eff/365. Exploratory link between spot moves and a near-ATM option IV; small negative corr on subsample.
used_to_decide: Documents IV vs spot co-movement on tape (weak negative OLS/corr in this subsample), kept as context for same thesis—no full delta-hedge leg per failed v1. v5 full-chain WLS was abandoned after backtest; v7 keeps closed 5-strike WLS and drops 5000 as trade candidate.

analysis_description: BS call **gamma** at IV inverted from mid (S=VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT mid, r=0, T=dte_eff/365) for VEV_5100..VEV_5500, subsampled every 200 timestamps on days 0–2. Mean gamma peaks near 5200/5300 in this strip (roughly 0.00224) and tapers toward wings; supports vega-scaled sizing and focusing on liquid ATM neighborhood.
used_to_decide: Reinforces that the 5100–5500 strip is the high-curvature region of the smile for the assigned spot range; paired with v8 EMA + book-depth gate to avoid acting on microstructure noise.

analysis_description: BS European **call theta** (per option per year in price space) at IV from mid for VEV_5100..VEV_5500, r=0, T=dte_eff/365, subsampled every 400 timestamps on days 0–2. Mean theta is most negative around 5200/5300 (magnitude ~1850 vs ~750 at 5500 in this subsample), consistent with higher time decay vega concentration near ATM.
used_to_decide: Documents time-decay profile on the traded strip; parameter sweeps v9 (vega-weighted WLS) and v10 (EMA/threshold) both underperformed v8, so we keep v8's linear book weighting in WLS for now.

analysis_description: Lag-1 autocorrelation of raw IV smile residuals per strike on VEV_5100..VEV_5500 (fit over same strip, spread<=8, all timestamps days 0-2). Residual persistence is very high (about 0.88 to 0.97), consistent with using an EMA residual signal rather than one-tick residual for entries.
used_to_decide: Confirms persistence in residual process (supports EMA entry smoothing from v8). Tested hydrogel risk wind-down in v11 to reduce day-2 drawdown risk, but total PnL dropped materially; retained v8 as best variant.

analysis_description: Cross-sectional pool (subsample every 100 ts): top-of-book spread vs |IV residual| on VEV_5100..VEV_5500 after OLS quadratic smile (no WLS) when all five pass spread<=8. Pool corr(spread,|res|) is moderately negative (~-0.50 on 1500 joint points), suggesting wider books often coincide with smaller cross-sectional mispricings in that snapshot; per-strike correlations are near zero in the same subsample.
used_to_decide: Exploratory microstructure context; v12 tested EMA innovation gating (from lag-1 persistence insight) but reduced voucher PnL vs v8 — keep v8 entry rule.

analysis_description: Family 10 cadence analysis on smile_quadratic_logm_wls: per-timestamp WLS quadratic coefficients (c2,c1,c0) over VEV_5100..VEV_5500, days 0-2. Coefficient drift is modest between adjacent ticks (median L2 ~0.014 at 100-tick cadence, ~0.017 at 500 ticks), suggesting slower/conditional refit may be viable.
used_to_decide: Implemented cadence variants fast(100), slow(500), conditional(300+gate) with same guardrails. Backtests + fill summaries were identical across all three, indicating this policy currently behaves as if cadence is a no-op (not a meaningful variation in this implementation regime).

analysis_description: Hard duplicate-check extension: event-triggered refit (|ΔS|>=2 ticks) with stale timeout over quadratic_logm_wls. Despite different logic and cadence machinery, backtest outputs, tradeHistory length, and symbol-level fill counts were exactly unchanged vs cadence runs and v8 baseline in this branch.
used_to_decide: This confirms cadence/refit variation is not expressing behavior under current guardrails/policy flow; high duplicate risk remains for this family variant unless assumptions are changed more fundamentally.

analysis_description: Deep underlying-to-voucher response analysis: first-difference beta/elasticity dV~dS per strike, lag-1 beta proxy, spread widening after |dS| shocks (>=2 ticks), and up/down asymmetry. Results ranked 5200/5300/5400 strongest on immediate response with relatively tight calm spreads. Added event gate using underlying shock + response rank.
used_to_decide: Incorporated suggested response-ranked symbols and shock gating directly into v17 entry logic. This produced materially different fill profile (e.g., VEV_5400 fill rows 823 -> 225) but lower total PnL than v8 under worse fills; retain v8 as best for now while keeping this regime mapping as a basis for future variants.

analysis_description: Mapped underlying→voucher response rank (5200/5300/5400 high, 5500 weaker on calm-spread+response score) to fixed size multipliers in response_score_to_size_mult.json. v18 applied these to the vega-based q; however integer rounding yields identical PnL and fill counts to v8 — multipliers are effectively no-ops for q at current vega/limits.
used_to_decide: Next iteration should use floor/ceil of adjusted q, or add partial fills via sub-tranches, to make size skew express in trades when desired.

analysis_description: Per-symbol predictive test: residual at t vs next-tick idiosyncratic move y=dV-beta*dS, where beta is contract-specific from OLS dV~dS. All traded strikes had negative slope y~resid; strongest at 5200/5100, weaker at 5400/5500. Derived entry thresholds from residual quantiles and side constraints from tail-bucket sign behavior.
used_to_decide: Used this to intentionally increase signal usage on 5100/5200 and side-restrict weaker symbols. This produced much higher voucher turnover (e.g., VEV_5300/5200/5100 fill rows increased sharply) but reduced total PnL vs v8, indicating overtrading/noise despite predictive signal direction.

analysis_description: Execution reality check: parsed v8/v18 tradeHistory — only rows with buyer or seller equal to SUBMISSION are our fills; they are **HYDROGEL_PACK** and **VEV_5400** only (other VEV lines in the log are other agents). underlying_to_voucher_response.json still motivates per-strike behavior, but only 5400 matters for our option PnL. Greek: 5400 remains an OTM call in the strip with meaningful vega (used in sizing) and moderate dV~dS.
used_to_decide: On |dS|>=2 (same 2-tick shock scale as the tape study), new 5400 entries use a one-tick-more-passive limit (off the touch toward mid) while keeping v8 EMA/residual/gates; flattening still lifts the touch. Improves total PnL vs v8 (13499 vs 13478) under worse fills — a small but real behavioral delta focused on the strike we actually trade.

analysis_description: Focused contract-level propagation study for the actually-traded voucher leg (VEV_5400): computed per-timestamp 5-strike WLS IV residuals, then regressed next-tick idiosyncratic move (dV-beta*dS) on residual, split by extract stress direction (|dS|>=2 with sign). Also compared next-tick spread conditional on stress-up/down. T uses round3description timing convention (dte_eff to years).
used_to_decide: For VEV_5400, residual predictiveness is stronger during stress-up than stress-down (slope -5.38 vs -3.83), while spread/topsum are similar. Implemented asymmetric stress quoting: on stress-up keep short-entry at touch for faster capture; on stress-down keep passive one-tick-off-touch behavior.

analysis_description: Read `round3work/vouchers_final_strategy/STRATEGY.txt` and `outputs/r3_tight_spread_summary.txt` (day 0: Welch t on K=20 forward extract mid, mean fwd higher when both s5200,s5300<=2; P(tight)~0.10; corr(s5200,s5300)~0.22). Re-ran a strict gate-only voucher variant and an EMA extract leg on 4 R3 days: large negative PnL under worse fills (mid story does not transfer without execution). Shipped v22 as v21 + soft 5200+5300 spread regime sizing on the existing volume-WLS residual fade; IV via BS inversion + WLS strip unchanged; Greek sizing still vega~20/q cap.
used_to_decide: Use joint tightness as a **Sonic** book-state modulator: tight both legs -> full q; else half-rounded q, but in practice identical to v21 at int(20/vega). Keep v21 5400 stress asymmetry. Record both `--match-trades worse` and `all` (identical PnL/fills this run). Next: try gate tied to *quoted* 5200/5300 size or explicit extract legs with spread-aware limits.
